project.description:
Test fixtures for pure-JavaScript code authored under the homing ssjs (server-side-JS) substrate.
Vends a GraalVM-Polyglot-backed JUnit harness so any pure JS module — Secretaries (RFC 0028), client-side reducers, validators, serialisation helpers — can be unit-tested on the JVM against a real ECMAScript runtime, without spinning up a browser or transpiling.
The harness is split into two layers:
- JsModuleTestBase — generic. Boots a JS context, loads a module from the classpath, looks up named exports, ships an envelope() helper that marshals Java Maps into native JS objects via ProxyObject, and tears the context down on @AfterEach.
- SecretaryTestBase — Diligent-Secretaries-doctrine layer. Adds the Step contract — initial(), dispatch(state, env) — and terse assertions over { newState, actions }.
Dependencies (GraalVM Polyglot + js-community) are exposed at compile scope intentionally: this module IS test fixtures, so a downstream test module pulling it in (typically at test scope from its own pom) automatically gets a working runtime with no further config.
Public downstream projects can depend on this module identically to any homing artifact; the test-fixtures naming makes the intent legible at the dependency-graph level (vs. opaque test-jar classifiers, which the dependency-as-test-jar refactor explicitly replaced).
